headNote: HEAD carries phase-000 baseline evidence only (census + gates); no renames. Tree at HEAD == BASE for all in-scope migration surfaces.

hardening.rootCause: system-spec-kit is an npm workspaces monorepo (workspaces: [shared, mcp_server, scripts]). Three manifests were untracked and therefore absent from a clean checkout: the workspace-root package.json, shared/package.json, scripts/package.json. Without the root manifest, npm ci reported 'could not read package.json' and mcp_server's file:../shared dependency could not resolve (install hung).
hardening.fix: Track the three untracked manifests. The workspace lockfile (system-spec-kit/package-lock.json) was already committed; the fresh install reproduced it byte-for-byte (blob 1f4017e931), which is strong determinism evidence. Per-package lockfiles from an earlier attempt were wrong for a workspaces layout and were removed.

reproReplay.command: rm -rf node_modules */dist && npm ci && npm run build
reproReplay.note: Deterministic install from the tracked lockfile reproduces a working toolchain.

recursiveStrictValidation.command: validate.sh <node> --strict over every node with spec.md
recursiveStrictValidation.note: Zero errors across the whole 020 tree. 34 nodes carry a single non-blocking PHASE_LINKS adjacency warning (sorted-order nav refs under 008-component-migration/{002-sk-design, 003-sk-doc/003-create-packets, 014-agents}); pre-existing at BASE, warnings are non-blocking per the plan.

laneC.note: BASE corpus IDs recorded for the phase-002 parity check (count + IDs unchanged after consumer migration).
